module.title: Week 16: SOC Operations & Alert Triage
module.focus: SOC tiers, escalation, KPIs, alert pipelines

topics.title: Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand SOC tiers
- Roles
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Draw a 3-tier SOC org chart and explain the responsibilities of each tier
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the three tiers of a SOC?
topics.interviewAnswer: Tier 1 (L1) triages alerts and escalates. Tier 2 (L2) investigates deeply using SIEM and endpoint forensics. Tier 3 (L3) does advanced malware analysis, reverse engineering, and threat hunting.

quiz.question: How many tiers in a typical SOC?
What does L1 do?
What does L2 do?
options: Deep investigation using SIEM and forensics

quiz.question: What does L3 do?
options: Advanced malware analysis and threat hunting

quiz.question: What was the Target breach lesson?
options: Clear tier responsibilities and escalation criteria are vital

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: SOC Organizational Structure can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: SOC Tiers & Escalation Procedures
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: SOC Tiers & Escalation Procedures is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Learn escalation paths
- SLAs
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Create an escalation flowchart with SLA times for each tier
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the difference between vertical and horizontal escalation?
topics.interviewAnswer: Vertical escalation moves up tiers (L1 to L2 to L3). Horizontal escalation moves to another team (e.g., network to endpoint). Both have distinct SLA requirements.

quiz.question: What does vertical escalation mean?
What does L1 do before escalating?
options: Enrich the alert with initial data

quiz.question: What SLA timer is critical?
What triggers escalation to L2?
What was the Colonial Pipeline lesson?
options: Proper escalation revealed ransomware in progress

topics.title: Day 3: SOC KPIs & Metrics
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: SOC KPIs & Metrics is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand SOC performance measurement
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Calculate MTTD and MTTR from raw data and design a KPI dashboard
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the difference between MTTD and MTTR?
topics.interviewAnswer: MTTD (Mean Time to Detect) measures how quickly a genuine threat is identified. MTTR (Mean Time to Respond) measures how long it takes to contain and remediate after detection.

quiz.question: What does MTTD measure?
options: Time to detect a genuine threat

quiz.question: What is a healthy MTTR target?
What does high false positive rate indicate?
What is alert volume?
What metric justifies SOC budget?
Which best describes Day 3: SOC KPIs & Metrics?
quiz.explanation: Day 3: SOC KPIs & Metrics is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 4: Alert Classification & Prioritization
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Alert Classification & Prioritization is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Master alert severity classification
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Create a severity matrix and classify sample alerts correctly
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the difference between severity and priority?
topics.interviewAnswer: Severity is based on technical impact (CVSS, asset value). Priority accounts for business context, such as whether the affected system handles PII or is customer-facing.

quiz.question: What factors determine severity?
What would be a Critical alert?
What is priority distinct from?
What alert was misclassified in SolarWinds?
What helps calculate priority?
Which best describes Day 4: Alert Classification & Prioritization?
quiz.explanation: Day 4: Alert Classification & Prioritization is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 5: Building Alert Pipelines
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Building Alert Pipelines is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Design automated alert pipelines
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a complete alert pipeline diagram with correlation rules
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the key stages of an alert pipeline?
topics.interviewAnswer: Log ingestion, normalization, filtering, correlation, enrichment, and alert generation. Each stage reduces noise and adds context before an analyst sees the alert.

quiz.question: What is the first stage of an alert pipeline?
What does normalization do?
options: Converts logs to a common schema

quiz.question: What is enrichment?
What does a correlation rule do?
What did the healthcare SOC pipeline correlate?
options: Failed VPN logins with badge access

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 5: Building Alert Pipelines?
